qa_pairs.question: What are some common connection errors users might encounter when connecting to a cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Users might encounter errors such as "no matching cipher found", "no matching MAC found", "unable to negotiate a key exchange method", "couldn't agree a key exchange algorithm", or "remote host identification has changed".

qa_pairs.question: What could cause the "remote host identification has changed" error message?
qa_pairs.answer: This error message can indicate a man-in-the-middle attack or an upgrade to the security of the cluster you are trying to connect to.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended action if you receive a "remote host identification has changed" error?
qa_pairs.answer: You should verify that the host key fingerprint mentioned in the message matches one of the host key fingerprints published at [[SSH host keys]].

qa_pairs.question: Is it safe to continue connecting if the host key fingerprint matches the published list after a "remote host identification has changed" error?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if the host key fingerprint matches one on the published list, it is safe to continue connecting.

qa_pairs.question: What should you do if the host key fingerprint does not match the published list when you encounter a "remote host identification has changed" error?
qa_pairs.answer: If the host key fingerprint does not appear on our published list, you should terminate the connection and [[Technical support|contact support]].
